title: hieracium maculatum subsp. celsicaule
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. celsicaule, commonly known as spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as wounds, fever, and skin infections. The medicinal properties of Hieracium maculatum subsp. celsicaule are attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which may also contribute to its potential health benefits, including immune system support and antioxidant activity. Hieracium maculatum subsp. celsicaule can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
